articles.title: Hako: A New High-Performance Embeddable JavaScript Engine
articles.description: A fork of PrimJS (which is, itself, built on top of QuickJS) that compiles down to WebAssembly and can act as a portable, embeddable JavaScript engine for other apps (here’s an example of using it in a Go app).
articles.author: Andrew Sampson

articles.title: A Flowing WebGL Gradient, Deconstructed
articles.description: Even if you don’t want to render a neat plasma-style effect on the Web, this is a wonderfully deep exploration of the math and technology behind doing so using simple GLSL code that could be easily understood by any JavaScript developer.
articles.author: Alex Harri

articles.title: Advanced React in the Wild
articles.description: A round-up of case studies showing how five different engineering teams have pushed React to the limit in production and their real-world wins in areas like performance, Core Web Vitals, caching, and more. A lot to enjoy here.
articles.author: Addy Osmani and Hassan Djirdeh

articles.description: Mozilla has enabled its Temporal implementation by default as of Firefox 139.

📘 Dr. Axel Rauschmayer has released Exploring TypeScript: TS 5.8 edition, a book bringing together all of his recent research into modern TypeScript development. You can buy the book but also read it all online for free in HTML form, if you prefer.
ESLint has added support for 'bulk suppressions', a way to make adopting stricter linting rules more manageable.
Dan Abramov is back with JSX Over The Wire, a fantastic meditation on the evolution of passing data and behavior between servers and clients and how things could get even better.

🤖 Microsoft's Burke Holland ▶️ shows off VS Code's new 'agent mode' feature which really makes Copilot far more powerful and a fine alternative to dedicated AI-powered editors. I've been using it a lot this week.
Google has made Firebase App Hosting generally available. It offers a streamlined, fully-managed deployment process for Angular and Next.js apps.
Sacha Greif, the name behind the State of JavaScript survey, has just launched the first 'State of Devs' survey. 🗳️ You can fill it out here.
Astro 5.7 – The popular content framework gains an experimental fonts API, its sessions API is now stable, and there's support for using local SVG files as components.
WebStorm 2025.1 – JetBrains' JavaScript IDE – fresh with big AI, Angular, monorepo, and Next.js enhancements.
tldts 7.0 – URL parsing library to extract domains, subdomains, suffixes, etc.
gridstack.js 12.0 – Build responsive interactive dashboards quickly.
Lexe – Package a Node app into a single, small executable.
DOCX 9.4 – Generate Word documents from JavaScript.
Redux Toolkit 2.7, Bun v1.2.10, Babylon.js 8.3, Rambda 10.0

articles.title: Default Styles for H1 Elements are Changing
articles.description: Browsers are updating the default user agent (UA) styles for nested section headings. This high-level change is something that you’re going to want to be aware of — otherwise you may find yourself scratching your head in a few months when certain H1 elements start behaving differently.
articles.author: Simon Pieters (MDN)

articles.title: Comparing Tauri and Electron for Building Desktop Apps with Frontend Technologies
articles.description: Electron is a natural choice for building JS and HTML-powered cross-platform desktop apps but numerous alternatives have appeared like Neutralinojs and the Rust-based Tauri. This post does a good job of quickly showing how Tauri differs and why you might choose it.
articles.author: Costa Alexoglou

articles.title: The Post-Developer Era
articles.description: Two years ago Josh published a piece on the rise of AI, and the fear and anxiety that devs were feeling at the time about AI taking jobs. Now, two years on, he’s back with a follow up to see how things have panned out so far, and how to now frame our thinking on these rapidly evolving set of tools and technologies.
articles.author: Josh W. Comeau

articles.title: Updates to the Customizable Select API
articles.description: This article was first published earlier this year, but is really worth checking out now as support for the feature has shipped in the recently released Chrome 135.
articles.author: Una Kravets

articles.title: Item Flow
articles.description: A couple of issues back we shared an article from Jen Simmons on the WebKit team about a new unifying layout module, bringing together flex-flow and grid-auto-flow. Here are Ahmad’s positive thoughts on the idea, along with some questions.
articles.author: Ahmad Shadeed

articles.title: Hiding Elements That Require JavaScript Without JavaScript
articles.description: If you’ve got non-essential features that require JavaScript and you want to hide them for users who have JavaScript disabled for whatever reason, this is a tidy old-school way to do it.
articles.author: Dade

articles.title: The CSS shape() Function
articles.description: Simon runs us through the shape() function and how we can use it to create shapes that “can adapt and scale with mixed proportions based on the size of the element”.
articles.author: Simon Fraser (WebKit)

articles.title: Using currentColor in 2025
articles.description: Sure, custom properties can do much the same, but Chris looks at the utility of this keyword alternative.
articles.author: Chris Coyier

articles.title: Next Level CSS Styling for Cursors
articles.description: Some styling pointers for custom cursors, along with what things to consider in regards to accessibility and fallbacks.
articles.author: Declan Chidlow

articles.title: Inclusive Dark Mode: Designing Accessible Dark Themes For All Users
articles.description: Getting a dark mode for a site just right is tricky at the best of times, so these tips on how to craft dark modes that work for everyone are most welcome.
articles.author: Alex Williams

articles.title: The Blissful Zen of a Good Side Project
articles.description: A reflective take on the importance of side projects. *nods*
articles.author: Josh Collinsworth

articles.title: Astro 5.7 Released
articles.description: This new release arrived just yesterday and it introduces SVG components, experimental Fonts API support, bug fixes, and more.
articles.author: Kane, Lefebvre, Stoppa, Moore (Astro)

articles.title: Font Tester: A Browser Extension to Live Test and Preview Google Fonts
articles.description: Opens a panel overlay that allows you to fiddle with fonts directly on the current page. A few neat features are the ability to preview multiple fonts or test a local custom font.
articles.author: Paul

articles.title: PureAnim: An SVG Animation Toolkit
articles.description: Just add an SVG and this tool lets you animate the different parts of the graphic via all sorts of settings in a timeline view. Choose from the prebuilt animations to see how it works.
articles.author: SEBASTIAN GROCHOCKI

articles.title: Carboard.js: A Lightweight, Reactive JavaScript Framework with a Twist
articles.description: The ‘twist’ is that you don’t have to write any HTML, CSS, or JSX (think VanJS). Other features include state management, routing, CSS-in-JS, and more.
articles.author: Manolo Edge

articles.title: Tailwind CSS Cheat Sheet: A Comprehensive Tailwind Cheat Sheet to Instantly Find All Utility Classes
articles.description: Covers Tailwind versions 3 and 4. The classes are organized using all the primary categories from the Tailwind docs and includes instant search.
articles.author: kombai
